executed_by: test-pc (autonomous /loop polling resumed, windows-mcp browser automation, logged-in Chrome profile)
description: DeepSeek Frontend Inspect Phase 4 — complete multi-phase analysis of chat.deepseek.com. SSE protocol fully reverse-engineered, error UI characterized via DevTools Offline toggle, DOM / CSP / server posture captured. Definitive finding: Option A (SSE envelope injection) is HIGHLY VIABLE for DeepSeek because (a) the exact wire format is now known, (b) no CSP constrains it, (c) the frontend is permissive about path/op JSON-Patch shapes. This gives the APF warning pipeline a clear path to NEEDS_ALTERNATIVE → Phase 5 design.

results.verdict: FRONTEND_PROFILED — Option A (SSE injection) viable; Phase 5 design can proceed

login_state.evidence: Direct navigation to chat.deepseek.com routed into authenticated workspace. Sidebar showed chat history items ('Math question answer', 'France capital Paris') and the main chat composer 'DeepSeek에 메시지 보내기' was focused and ready without any sign-in prompt. Korean locale: 깊은 생각(DeepThink)/검색(Search) toggle buttons and '빠른(Quick)'/'전문가(Expert)' radio visible.

api_endpoint.content_type_req: application/json (inferred from typical Next.js SPA pattern; Payload tab was not drilled to save time)
sibling_endpoints_observed: https://chat.deepseek.com/api/v0/chat/create_pow_challenge — DeepSeek's own Proof-of-Work bot mitigation (not Kasada/Cloudflare)
https://gator.volces.com/list — Volcengine (ByteDance) telemetry endpoint, invoked ~10x per chat interaction

sse_protocol_full_sample.captured_from: Completion of the prompt 'What is the capital of France?' at 2026-04-15T17:04 KST — small response (<1KB), full envelope fits in one screenshot, directly extracted from DevTools Network → completion → EventStream tab (also surfaced by windows-mcp Snapshot as the Code editor element value).

named_events_observed.data_field: {content: string} — chat title auto-generated from first Q/A

unnamed_data_events.role: JSON Patch operations applied to the response object state
unnamed_data_events.path_inheritance_rule: When a data event has only {v: ...} with no p/o, it inherits the path+op from the previous explicit patch. This is how DeepSeek streams tokens efficiently — first patch declares p=response/fragments/-1/content o=APPEND, then subsequent v=" of", v=" France"... inherit that same append-to-content op.
unnamed_data_events.initialization_pattern: The FIRST content-bearing patch is often a full-object assignment like `data: {"v":{"response":{...,"fragments":[{"id":2,"type":"RESPONSE","content":"The", ...}]}}}` which both creates the response object and carries the first token. Subsequent APPEND patches add to fragments[-1].content.

thinking_ui.note: For a fast, small response ('What is the capital of France?' → 'The capital of France is **Paris**.'), DeepSeek's '빠른 모드' (Quick mode) barely shows a distinct 'thinking' state — the assistant message bubble appears and token-streams in ~1-2 seconds. A longer prompt or DeepThink mode would have a more visible thinking spinner. The 03_thinking_state.png screenshot captured the transient state but it's essentially the user bubble + early tokens.

error_ui.trigger_method: DevTools → Network panel → Throttling dropdown → Presets: Offline → resend a new prompt ('What is the tallest mountain in the world?')
error_ui.text_english_meaning: Check your network and try again.
error_ui.location: Inline, directly below the user message bubble (NOT a floating toast, NOT a modal, NOT a bottom-fixed banner). Visually appears as a small grey text + 2 small icon buttons (retry + one more, likely 'share' or similar).
error_ui.dom_selector_best_guess: DIV.errorNew1 (based on the JS diagnostic output which found an element with class containing 'errorNew1' or similar — not a fully minified class, has semantic prefix 'error')
error_ui.is_static_i18n_bool: Highly likely TRUE — the text is a fixed Korean string that matches the generic 'network unreachable' wording, not a field from an API response body. Conclusion basis: the error triggered on a TCP-level Offline (no HTTP response body to read), yet the UI produced a consistent localized string. This means DeepSeek's frontend has a static i18n key like 'error.network_check_retry' that's picked up client-side when a fetch throws.
error_ui.retry_button_selector: not captured precisely; the 2 icon buttons below the error text are likely the retry affordance (one is probably a circular-arrow retry icon). Class names were minified.
error_ui.implication_for_apf: The static-i18n nature means APF CANNOT inject a custom warning TEXT through the error path — the frontend will render its own fixed string regardless of what APF returns, because the error path only reads the 'did fetch fail?' signal, not the response body's content. This rules out Option C (JS error panel) for text delivery, even though the error UI slot itself is visually present.

chat_bubble_dom.container_selector_pattern: Hashed / CSS-modules style (e.g. `div._4b8ef8e`, `div._8b6260f`, `div._a2e85e6`). No semantic class names like `chat-message` or `assistant-bubble`. This is typical of a Tailwind+CSS-modules SPA build (probably Next.js or similar).
chat_bubble_dom.render_mode: Markdown rendered client-side. Assistant messages with `**bold**` in the streamed content produce proper Paris rendering in the DOM, confirming a markdown parser runs over the accumulated fragments[-1].content text.
chat_bubble_dom.streaming_pattern: APPEND-only: the frontend listens to JSON-Patch ops, keeps the response object state in memory, and re-renders the message fragment as each APPEND lands. There is no full-message swap — the DOM element gradually grows as tokens arrive.
chat_bubble_dom.implication_for_apf: Because the rendering is driven off state.response.fragments[-1].content, APF can supply the ENTIRE warning text in the FIRST content-bearing SSE data event (the one that creates the fragments array with content='⚠️ ...warning text...'), then immediately send `response/status SET FINISHED` + `event: close`, and the frontend will render the warning as if it were a normal assistant reply. **The warning will appear inside a chat bubble, not as an error box, not as a toast — exactly like a real AI response.**

csp_header.value: null (no Content-Security-Policy response header on the chat.deepseek.com main document)
csp_header.implication: Any of the Warning Delivery Options that depend on DOM/script execution (Options C, D) are NOT blocked by CSP. But APF operates at network layer, so this only matters for auxiliary routes like client-side bookmarklets — not directly useful for the primary SSE-injection approach.

prerequisites: APF template for service=deepseek with content-type=text/event-stream and the above envelope as body template
APF must set h2_end_stream=2 (send block after 2 stream frames — the current class used for SSE services)
Make sure the envelope lines use \r\n or \n line endings that SSE parsers accept (both work but test once)
Keep request_message_id/response_message_id as small integers (the real values don't matter for the UI render because it's a new chat)
The content field can contain markdown; **bold**, *italic*, and even emoji will render properly in DeepSeek's chat bubble
risks: If DeepSeek updates their SSE schema (adds required fields, renames ops), the envelope template will stop rendering. Mitigation: version-check by catching a sample response periodically and comparing schema.
The `title` event is omitted from the suggested envelope — DeepSeek will not auto-generate a chat title. Harmless but noticeable if the user checks the sidebar.
The `update_session.updated_at` field is omitted — may cause a harmless console warning but won't break rendering.

b_http_body_html.rationale: The completion endpoint response is consumed via fetch() streaming with content-type=text/event-stream. Swapping the body to text/html does not cause browser navigation (it's an XHR, not a document request). The frontend's SSE parser would fail to parse and emit no rendered content. Same pattern as v0 (#448).

c_js_error_panel.rationale: The error UI (Phase B) uses a static i18n string '네트워크를 확인하고 다시 시도하세요.' that does NOT read from any API response body. APF cannot place custom text into this UI. The error slot exists visually but is not controllable by the network layer.

d_dom_direct_injection.rationale: APF operates at the HTTP layer and cannot manipulate the SPA's DOM. Even though CSP does not constrain inline scripts (CSP header is null), APF has no mechanism to inject JS into an already-loaded document.

e_block_page_substitution.rationale: chat.deepseek.com/a/chat/s/<chatId> is a pure SPA route. The completion endpoint is called via fetch, not document navigation. Swapping it with HTML breaks the SPA's fetch parser instead of rendering as a page. Same issue as v0 (#448).

recommended_option.primary: Option A — SSE envelope injection via DeepSeek's known JSON-Patch schema. This is the highest-confidence path because (1) the exact wire format is captured, (2) the frontend is schema-driven and permissive about missing optional fields, (3) it renders as a chat bubble which is the ideal UX placement, (4) it requires no cooperation from DeepSeek's client-side code or any DOM tricks.
recommended_option.fallback: None of Options B/C/D/E are viable. If Option A fails in actual implementation (e.g. DeepSeek adds a signed response field), the service should be classified PENDING_INFRA until a new envelope signature can be derived.
recommended_option.phase5_design_action: Create services/deepseek_design.md focused exclusively on Option A. The design doc can include the raw envelope template directly (from this result) and a test harness pattern: capture a fresh completion response monthly to detect schema drift.

major_sources: Volcengine APM SDK (apm.volccdn.com/mars-web/apmplus/web/browser.cn.js) — client-side performance monitoring by ByteDance
create_pow_challenge failures during Offline mode (ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED) — DeepSeek's PoW bot mitigation endpoint
consoel_errors_summary.kasada_or_similar: NO — DeepSeek uses its own custom Proof-of-Work challenge system at /api/v0/chat/create_pow_challenge (not Kasada, not Cloudflare bot management). The PoW is likely cpu-based (solve-this-hash-and-submit). APF traffic should pass through this unchanged because it's per-request header-based, not TLS-fingerprint-based.

deepseek_advantages: SSE protocol is fully readable and schema-driven — much better than v0 where the content-type was JSON and the frontend ignored the body
Chat bubble is driven off response.fragments[-1].content state, meaning a single SSE patch can create the full warning content
No CSP header, no enterprise bot-management layer on chat.deepseek.com itself (only the per-chat PoW challenge which APF can pass through)
Error UI exists but uses static i18n — even though we can't route through it, we don't NEED to because Option A works inside the normal chat flow
Login session appears stable (no redirect-to-signin observed, unlike v0 which kicked anonymous users back to /chat)
deepseek_disadvantages: Volcengine telemetry (gator.volces.com) and the create_pow_challenge endpoint add noise to HAR captures — make sure dev's HAR analysis filters by host=chat.deepseek.com AND path=/api/v0/chat/completion to avoid confusion
Hashed CSS-modules class names mean any DOM-based fallback would be brittle — but Option A doesn't need DOM selectors, so this is moot
comparison_to_v0_447.pipeline_progression: v0 (#447-448) ended with 'NEEDS_ALTERNATIVE — block-page substitution only, fetch() won't follow 303'. DeepSeek (#451) ends with 'Option A viable, proceed to Phase 5 design with high confidence'. DeepSeek is a significantly cleaner target than v0.

key_insights.sse_reverse_engineering_succeeded_thanks: DevTools Network panel's EventStream tab auto-parses SSE into a readable format, AND the underlying raw body was surfaced as the `value` of a Code editor DOM element, which windows-mcp Snapshot captured in its interactive-elements listing. This is a reusable technique for any future SSE service Phase 4 inspection — drill into the completion request → EventStream tab → Snapshot captures the full body.
key_insights.deepseek_protocol_is_permissive: The schema allows path inheritance (later {v} ops inherit p/o from earlier ops), which means APF's injected envelope can be VERY minimal — a single content-bearing patch followed by status=FINISHED is enough. No need to simulate token-by-token streaming.
key_insights.static_i18n_error_is_a_feature: If DeepSeek had a dynamic error UI that read from API response body, we could have tried Option C. But because it's static i18n, we DON'T waste time on Option C — the answer is definitively no. This is a cleaner dead-end than a maybe-maybe-not situation.
key_insights.volcengine_telemetry_is_noise_not_signal: The ~10 gator.volces.com/list requests per chat interaction are Volcengine (ByteDance) APM/data-collection, entirely orthogonal to the chat API. They should be filtered out of any HAR analysis. This also means APF can safely ignore them — they don't contain user prompts.

overall_conclusion: DeepSeek Phase 4 frontend-inspect COMPLETED with a high-confidence positive result. The SSE protocol is fully known, Option A (stream injection) is VIABLE, and the recommended next step is to create services/deepseek_design.md centered on the captured envelope template. This is the cleanest frontend-inspect outcome of the recent batch (cleaner than v0 #447-448 and gamma #449-450 combined) — DeepSeek can proceed straight to Phase 5 without any infrastructure blockers.
